_risk_level_doc: Change is confined to two configuration/command files. No security boundary is crossed; only additive policy grants (new allowed prefix, no removal of denials). Hook logic is unchanged.

requirement.original: Codex CLI invocations from subagents currently lack a canonical writable output path. When subagents invoke the codex Skill, Codex output cannot be directed to a file because tool-policy.v1.json does not grant write access to a codex output directory — resulting in a 'no-tee workaround' where Codex output only goes to stdout. The desired end-state is: docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt is the canonical Codex output path; tool-policy.v1.json grants write access to that directory prefix for the relevant roles; and wherever the codex Skill definition lives (commands/, SKILL.md, or equivalent), the default output target reflects that canonical path. After this change, subagents can write Codex output to a file in docs/codex/ without being blocked by hooks, and the no-tee workaround is retired.
requirement.clarified: Add */docs/codex/ to allowed_write_path_prefixes for 12 Codex-consultation roles in tool-policy.v1.json; update commands/codex.md Step 2 and Step 4 to use docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt as the canonical default path with deterministic derivation of task_id and role; demote /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ to exceptional fallback only.
requirement.what: Grant write access to docs/codex/ directory prefix and establish it as the canonical default Codex output path in the codex command definition
requirement.why: Subagents invoking the codex Skill cannot currently write output to a persistent, structured file without being blocked by the tool policy hook; this forces a workaround where output goes only to stdout

included: Adding */docs/codex/ to allowed_write_path_prefixes for ba, qa, dev, pm, architect, product-owner, cleanliness-inspector, prompt-inspector, spec, style-inspector, ui-specialist, user
Incrementing policy_version to 4 with _note explaining the change
Updating commands/codex.md Step 2 to generate docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt as canonical path
Updating commands/codex.md Step 4 to use a hook-resolvable tee target
excluded: Modifying any hook file or lib/ Python modules
Creating docs/codex/ directory in git (runtime mkdir)
Adding docs/codex/ to timeline, transportation, test-writer, general-purpose, changelog-analyst, push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst

success_criteria: commands/codex.md Step 2 generates docs/codex/<task_id>/<role>.txt as primary path
bash tee target in commands/codex.md Step 4 is hook-resolvable (not an opaque shell variable)
ba-role subagent Bash write to docs/codex/20260521-090100/ba.txt is not blocked
constraints: Do not modify hook files or lib/ Python modules
Policy path prefix must use */docs/codex/ glob form (not /docs/codex/ anchored or docs/codex/ bare)
Tee target in codex.md must resolve without arbitrary shell variable expansion by the hook parser

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py (read only — understand matching behavior; do not modify)
hooks/lib/bash_write_targets.py (read only — understand _resolve_path behavior for AC4)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Subagents invoking codex Skill cannot write output to docs/codex/ because the path is absent from allowed_write_path_prefixes in all role entries
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: tool-policy.v1.json was never updated to include a docs/codex/ write prefix; commands/codex.md was written before the canonical output path requirement existed
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a missing-feature gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ path was chosen for disk-backed storage to avoid tmpfs ENOSPC; no structured per-task-per-role path was designed at that time
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Codex output is ephemeral and not discoverable by task/role; no persistent audit trail exists in the repo; the tee workaround in codex.md cannot succeed because the policy blocks the write path
root_cause_analysis.timeline: 2026-05-21 — first time this gap is being addressed; no prior attempts
observations: grep 'docs/codex' policies/tool-policy.v1.json returns 0 matches (inferred: confirmed by file read lines 1-941)
commands/codex.md Step 2 generates /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ path, no mention of docs/codex/ (inferred: confirmed by file read lines 82-90)

BA subagent attempt to tee to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ during this very session was BLOCKED by pretool-tool-policy.py with 'write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes' (OBSERVED: live hook rejection during this analysis session)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete — live hook rejection observed during session
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: BA subagent received BLOCKED by tool-policy.v1 error when attempting Bash tee to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ in this session; policy file read confirmed no docs/codex/ prefix for any role
root_cause_analysis.located_source: policies/tool-policy.v1.json (all role entries, lines 14-941) and commands/codex.md (Step 2, lines 82-90)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not applicable — missing feature, not a regression

development_approach.strategy: Additive policy change: insert */docs/codex/ into each of the 12 consultation roles' allowed_write_path_prefixes array, increment policy_version, update _note. Then update commands/codex.md Step 2 to generate a structured canonical path and Step 4 to use a hook-resolvable tee target.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 actionable findings and 1 observation. Findings: (1) role exclusion reason was imprecise — timeline/transportation DO have Skill in the policy, so exclusion must be justified by 'not a codex-consultation role per agents/*.md', not 'no Skill tool'; (2) Bash tee variable expansion ambiguity — hook parser cannot expand arbitrary $VAR, so tee target must be a literal or $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR form; (3) task_id/role derivation must be explicitly specified in codex.md; (4) /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ should be exceptional fallback, not co-equal default. Observation: */docs/codex/ is the correct glob pattern.

findings.issue: Role exclusion reason was wrong: timeline/transportation DO have Skill in tool-policy.v1.json; test-writer and general-purpose also have Skill. Exclusion reason stated was 'no Skill tool' which is factually incorrect.
findings.proposed_fix: Reframe exclusion criterion as 'not a Codex-consultation role per agents/*.md definition'. The 12-role list itself is correct; only the stated justification needed correction.
findings.rationale: Spec and context JSON now state the correct exclusion criterion: consultation role identity, not Skill tool presence.

findings.issue: Bash tee + variable expansion: hook parser (lib/bash_write_targets.py _resolve_path) only expands $HOME, ~, $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — not arbitrary $CODEX_OUT. If codex.md Step 4 uses tee '$CODEX_OUT' with $CODEX_OUT set in a prior statement, hook sees literal '$CODEX_OUT' and blocks.
findings.proposed_fix: Update codex.md Step 4 to use a literal path or $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR-prefixed form directly in the tee command, not via an opaque shell variable. Add AC4 verifying hook-resolvable tee target.
findings.rationale: AC4 added; Technical Hints explain the _resolve_path constraint. Dev must restructure Steps 2-4 to inline the canonical path.

findings.issue: Step 2 must define how task_id and role are derived; otherwise agents may literally use '<task_id>' as a placeholder.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify in codex.md: task_id from $TASK_ID env or dev-registry session context; role from $ROLE env or resolved agent type; fallback to timestamp-only when absent.
findings.rationale: Added to Should Have requirements and to AC3 description.

findings.issue: Keeping /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ as co-equal fallback weakens 'canonical path as default' requirement.
findings.proposed_fix: Make docs/codex/ the mandatory default; /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ only for exceptional cases (mkdir failure, missing context) with explicit announcement.
findings.rationale: AC6 and Won't Have section updated; Should Have item added for explicit fallback documentation.

findings.issue: */docs/codex/ is the correct glob pattern and is consistent with existing policy patterns (OBSERVATION_ONLY).

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 4 actionable findings: corrected role exclusion rationale, added AC4 for hook-resolvable tee target, specified task_id/role derivation in requirements, demoted /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ to exceptional fallback.
